payload.text:
**Defining the React Application**

I'm solidifying the technical stack; React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS seem like a strong foundation. The `@google/genai` package for Gemini integration is planned for the intelligent todo management features. I'm brainstorming the "Smart Todo" functionality - AI-driven suggestions, priority assessments, and task breakdown are core concepts.


**Expanding the Application Features**

I'm now fully immersed in the feature set. I'm focusing on "Smart Breakdown," "Priority Suggester," and "Time Estimation" powered by Gemini. Dark mode and a sleek, responsive UI are definitely priorities. Local storage and the ability to add categories/tags are also in the design. I'm also now building the project structure and will begin implementation.


**Incorporating User Interaction**

I'm now fully immersed in the design details. I'm focusing on user interactions within the todo list, like checkboxes and delete buttons, and smooth transitions via Tailwind. I'm using simple SVGs for the icons. I'm also now focusing on refining the prompts for Gemini, and fixing any errors. I'm also ensuring that `process.env.API_KEY` is being used correctly, and that any code follows any project directives.

entries.description: Application metadata specifying name and description.
Primary HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font Awesome for icons.
Application entry point using React 18+ createRoot API.
Shared TypeScript interfaces and enums for the todo application.
Service layer for interacting with the Google Gemini API to provide smart task features.
Component representing an individual todo item with subtask support and AI features.
Input component with smart priority suggestion.
Sidebar/Panel for AI-driven productivity insights and motivational quotes.
Main application component managing global state and layout.

payload.text:
**Removing AI Integration**

Okay, I'm now focusing on removing all AI components from "ZenPlan AI Todo." I've identified key files: `index.html` with the title, `metadata.json` with the name and description mentioning AI, and `types.ts`. I will need to make changes to remove all references to the AI, namely Gemini, in the identified files.


**Eliminating AI Dependencies**

I'm now zeroing in on removing the AI elements. Specifically, I plan to delete `services/geminiService.ts` outright, as it's purely AI-focused, and remove all imports of it in other files. I will also modify the `components/TodoItem.tsx`, `components/TodoInput.tsx`, and `App.tsx` files to get rid of any AI-related UI elements like the "AI Breakdown" and "Smart Priority" buttons, and remove the rendering of the `AIAssistant` component.


**Refining Interface Elements**

I'm removing the `AIAssistant` component and the "Smart Priority" button and "AI Breakdown" button. I will also change the header title to "ZenPlan". I'm updating `metadata.json` to reflect the new name, "ZenPlan - Modern Todo", and a description focused on simplicity. I've updated the title in `index.html` to "ZenPlan Todo". I also need to remove `aiInsights` from `Todo` type and the `AIAnalysis` interface in `types.ts`, and clean up related state and functions in `components/TodoItem.tsx` and `components/TodoInput.tsx`.


**Adding Manual Priority Selection**

I'm now integrating a manual priority selector into the UI as a replacement for the removed "Smart Priority" button. This involves modifications to `TodoInput.tsx` to include the selector. In `App.tsx`, I'm removing the `AIAssistant` component, adjusting the grid layout to accommodate a wider list (and keeping the "Quick Stats"). I'm confident in the approach.

entries.description: Update application name and description to remove AI references.
Update page title and remove AI-related branding.
Clean up types to remove AI-specific fields.
Remove AI Breakdown functionality and simplify the item component.
Remove Smart Priority feature and add manual priority selection.
Remove AI Assistant and update the layout to a cleaner, non-AI version.
